commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=33702de3cebd377b59d3fb1bb49c55c275893da8)
commands.review_summary:
The PR normalizes MiniMax OAuth token expiry values during login and adds MiniMax `prepareRuntimeAuth` hooks that return the resolved credential for runtime auth state caching.

Reproducibility: Partly yes by source inspection: current main stores MiniMax token endpoint `expired_in` directly into OAuth `expires`, while the auth store expects an absolute millisecond timestamp. I did not establish a live Windows/Feishu/MiniMax CN reproduction, and the API-key mode slowdown remains unproven by source here.
commands.review_followup: Contributor and maintainer follow-up is needed because ClawSweeper cannot supply the required real setup proof and the auth hook shape needs review before merge.

target.title: fix(minimax): normalize OAuth token expiry and add prepareRuntimeAuth cache
target.body:
## Summary

- Converts MiniMax OAuth token `expired_in` from relative seconds to absolute millisecond timestamp, fixing `hasUsableOAuthCredential()` always treating the token as expired
- Adds `prepareRuntimeAuth` hook to both `minimax` (API key) and `minimax-portal` (OAuth) providers so the auth controller caches resolved credentials in `runtimeAuthState`

## Root Cause

MiniMax returns `expired_in` from the token endpoint as a relative duration in seconds (standard OAuth `expires_in` semantics), but the auth profile store's `hasUsableOAuthCredential()` expects an absolute millisecond timestamp. Without conversion, the token is always considered expired, triggering a slow OAuth refresh network call to `api.minimaxi.com` on every request. Combined with CN network latency (~18s per connection), this causes the 30-50s auth delay reported in the issue.

## Changes

**`extensions/minimax/oauth.ts`**: Added `normalizeOAuthExpires()` that handles three possible `expired_in` formats:
- Relative duration in seconds (< 1B): `Date.now() + expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute Unix timestamp in seconds (1B–1T): `expiredIn * 1000`
- Absolute millisecond timestamp (>= 1T): use as-is

**`extensions/minimax/provider-registration.ts`**: Added `prepareRuntimeAuth` to both providers. The hook returns `{ apiKey }` directly (no token exchange needed for MiniMax), which signals the auth controller to cache the credential in `runtimeAuthState` and avoid repeated profile resolution when the credential is still valid.

## Verification

- [x] MiniMax extension tests: 107 pass
- [x] Auth controller tests: 7 pass
- [x] Auth profile tests: 70 pass
- [x] MiniMax contract tests: 7 pass
- [x] Build succeeds
- [x] Formatting clean

Fixes #83449.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: nianjiuzst
